title: AB Brain v2 — System Overview
content:
# AB Brain v2 — System Overview

## Goal

A personal executive-function assistant for an ADHD + busy user. Reads from every meaningful input stream (email, calendar, meetings, notes, health, fitness, web), distills signal from noise, models the user, surfaces unseen connections, and triggers actions that Claude Code executes. Acts like a coach, not a search engine.

## Four-layer model

```
4. ACTION         Claude Code + custom skills (interactive + scheduled)
                       ▲ reads pending intents via MCP, executes, logs back
3. INFERENCE      Insight engine — six flavors of unseen connection
                       ▲ reads memory + user model
2. UNDERSTANDING  user_model — preferences, patterns, working style, identity
                       ▲ derived continuously from memory
1. MEMORY         Distilled records across all sources
                       ▲ Pass A
```

## The split

**AB Brain owns layers 1–3 plus an `intents` table that triggers actions.** Claude Code + skills owns layer 4. AB Brain knows *what should happen*; Claude Code *does it*. The split collapses scope dramatically — AB Brain doesn't need to build agent execution, draft-review UI, or undo logic. Claude Code already has tool use, permission prompts, and skills as the unit of capability.

## Build sequence

- **Track 1 (shipped April 2026):** security + integrity hardening — purge SQL hardened, header-only auth, Helmet CSP + CORS allowlist, transactions across multi-table writes. Baseline for everything below.
- **Pass A (months 1–2):** distillation pipeline + first 2 connectors (Gmail confirmed; second TBD).
- **Pass B (months 2–3):** pgvector embeddings on distilled records, entity table, insight engine, MCP server stood up.
- **Pass C (months 3–4):** user_model table, confidentiality tagging (whole-life coach makes this load-bearing), invalidate-don't-overwrite for evolving decisions.
- **Pass D (month 3+, parallel):** intents table, policy enforcer, first 2–3 Claude Code skills shipping. First action skills go live.

## Decided design choices

- Interaction surfaces: Claude.ai chat, Claude Projects, Claude Code, AB Brain PWA. All four read/write the brain via MCP (PWA via direct REST).
- Trigger mode: **background-scheduled** by default (most agentic), with strict per-skill safety tiers.
- Coach scope: **whole-life** (work + health + relationships).
- Coach tone: **balanced** — observes alignment between stated and revealed, names misalignment without preaching.
- LLM pipeline: keep OpenAI for default distillation/embedding. Sensitive (`restricted` tag) content bypasses OpenAI entirely; optional local-model lane for those.

## Why this shape

Most personal-AI products fail at layer 4 — they hallucinate actions and burn user trust. By delegating layer 4 to Claude Code (which already has interactive permission prompts, undo-friendly defaults, skills as scoped units), AB Brain only has to do the hard cognitive work and emit clean intents. Layer 4 ships in weeks, not months, once the foundation lands.

## Pointers to the other entries

- Memory: see *Memory: Distillation Pipeline* and *Memory: Storage Shape*
- Understanding: *Understanding: user_model*
- Inference: *Inference: Insight Engine*
- Action: *Action: Intents + Claude Code Skills*
- Coach posture: *Coach: Posture and Prompt*
- Open questions + roadmap: *Build Sequence + Open Questions*

title: AB Brain v2 — Memory: Distillation Pipeline
content:
# Memory Layer: Distillation Pipeline

## The actual hard problem

Connectors (Gmail, M365, Teams, Calendar, Apple Notes, Notion-read, Apple Health) are necessary plumbing but not the work. The work is the **transformation layer between raw stream and stored memory**. Without it, every new source makes the brain *worse* — more haystack, less needle.

Three jobs:

1. **Filter noise.** Newsletters, automated alerts, calendar reminders, "thanks!" replies, FYI CCs you didn't engage with. Don't store at all (or store in a quarantine table the AI ignores by default).
2. **Distill signal into a context-rich condensed form.** Per item or per thread — extract substance, decisions, commitments, participants, project/topic anchor, "why it matters." Drop the rest. Keep a pointer to the raw if ever needed.
3. **Store with retrieval handles.** Structured fields plus dense summary text — both LLM and search find it later.

## Pipeline

```
RAW ITEM
    ↓
PRE-FILTER (rule-based, free)
  - sender / domain blocklist
  - subject patterns ([AUTO], OOO, etc.)
  - length floor (<140 chars body)
  - participation signal (CC-only, no engagement)
    ↓
  ├─ NOISE → discard or quarantine
  ├─ SIGNAL → distill
  └─ AMBIGUOUS → LLM scoring
        ↓
      one cheap gpt-4o-mini call: signal Y/N + confidence
        ↓
      threshold: signal && confidence >= 0.6 → distill, else discard

DISTILLATION (per-source extractor)
  - email: per-thread
  - calendar: per-meeting
  - Teams: per-channel-per-day
  - notes: per-note
  - transcripts: per-conversation
    ↓
  output: structured memory record (see *Memory: Storage Shape*)
    ↓
STORE in memories table
```

## Pre-filter heuristics (start simple, tune)

Drop without LLM call when:
- Sender domain in blocklist (newsletters, marketing automation)
- Sender pattern: `noreply`, `no-reply`, `notifications@`, `automated@`
- Subject contains `[AUTO]`, `Out of office`, `Reminder:`, `Your weekly summary`
- Body shorter than 140 chars AND no participants beyond user
- Calendar invite with no description and only user as attendee
- Reply chain that is purely "thanks", "got it", "ok", "+1"

Pass to LLM scoring when:
- CC'd with no user reply
- Substantive body but ambiguous topic
- Forwarded thread without clear personal context

## LLM scoring prompt template

```
You are filtering [USER]'s inbox. Given this {email|note|message},
return JSON:
{
  "signal": true|false,
  "confidence": 0.0-1.0,
  "reason": "one short sentence"
}
Signal = the user will care about this in 6 months OR there's a
decision/commitment/question they need to know about.
Noise = newsletter-grade information, low-context updates,
ambient social noise.
```

## Per-source extractors

Each source has its own distillation prompt because the structure differs:

- **Email:** thread-level. Extract decisions, tasks, directives, perspectives, open questions. Participants from headers + signature lines.
- **Calendar:** meeting-level. Attendees, agenda items, prior meetings on same topic, decisions if minutes attached. Feeds `meeting-prep-digest` skill.
- **Teams / chat:** channel-day rollup. Notable threads, decisions, who said what. High noise, lower per-message value.
- **Bee transcripts:** existing route `routes/bee.js` already wires ingestion. Add the same extraction layer over transcript text.
- **Apple Notes:** captured via iOS Shortcuts → POST. Light filter, distill the substance.
- **Notion read:** pull page-by-page. Especially valuable for the LinkedIn-content stash.
- **Health/fitness:** structured already; not distilled, just stored. Becomes input to inference layer for correlations.

## Cost discipline

Distillation = one LLM call per signal item. Aggressive noise filter keeps this at ~20–50 calls/day for an active user → pennies/day at gpt-4o-mini. Permissive filter could be 500+ calls/day → a few dollars. Configurable per-source.

## Where this lands in code

- New module `lib/distillation/` with sub-modules per source
- `lib/distillation/filter.js` — pre-filter heuristics
- `lib/distillation/score.js` — LLM scoring wrapper
- `lib/distillation/extractors/{email,calendar,teams,note,transcript}.js`
- Each connector (`routes/connectors/gmail.js` etc.) calls into the pipeline
- Existing `routes/intake.js:201–263` is the prototype — stays for free-text manual input

## Build order

1. Build the distillation primitive **with manual paste as input** — copy/paste an email, produce the structured record, store. 2–4 days of prompt iteration on real samples.
2. Then wire Gmail as the first connector (3–5 days).
3. Add the noise pre-filter and LLM scoring (built into the pipeline from the start, but tuned with real data after Gmail flows).
4. Then M365 + Calendar + Teams (duplicates of step 2 with different APIs).

title: AB Brain v2 — Memory: Storage Shape
content:
# Memory Layer: Storage Shape

## The memory record

A signal item, post-distillation, looks like this:

```json
{
  "id": "uuid",
  "source": "gmail",
  "source_id": "thread_abc123",
  "occurred_at": "2026-04-25T14:30Z",
  "participants": ["alice@…", "bob@…", "user"],
  "subject": "Q2 offsite venue",
  "summary": "Alice proposed 3 venues. Bob preferred X for AV. Decision: X. User to book by Fri.",
  "decisions": [
    {"what": "Q2 offsite venue: X", "by": "alice+bob", "rationale": "AV setup", "when": "2026-04-25"}
  ],
  "tasks": [
    {"what": "book venue X", "owner": "user", "by": "2026-05-01", "auto_create": true}
  ],
  "directives": [
    {"from": "alice", "to": "user", "what": "loop in finance before booking", "by": null}
  ],
  "perspectives": [
    {"who": "user", "topic": "venue selection", "stance": "AV is the deciding factor", "confidence": "stated"}
  ],
  "questions_open": ["is finance OK with budget at venue X?"],
  "entities": ["Q2 offsite", "Alice", "Bob", "venue X", "finance"],
  "topics": ["q2-planning", "offsite-logistics"],
  "raw_ref": "gmail://thread/abc123",
  "confidentiality": "open",
  "confidence": "confirmed",
  "embedding": "<vector(1536) — Pass B>",
  "invalidated_at": null,
  "supersedes": null
}
```

## The five extracted dimensions

Each enables a different retrieval pattern:

| Dimension | Retrieval question it answers |
|---|---|
| **decisions** | "What did we decide about X?" → instant from a structured field |
| **tasks** | "What am I on the hook for?" → can auto-flow into existing `tasks` table with backref |
| **directives** | "What was I told to do, by whom?" → distinct from tasks |
| **perspectives** | "What does each person think about X?" → snapshot at a moment, becomes input for evolving-perspectives flavor of inference |
| **questions_open** | "What's still unresolved?" → loose threads the system can resurface |

## Schema landing — extend `knowledge` with a `kind` discriminator

Reuse existing search/activity-log/tag infrastructure. Add columns:

```sql
ALTER TABLE knowledge
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS kind TEXT DEFAULT 'note',
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS source_id TEXT,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS occurred_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS participants JSONB DEFAULT '[]',
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS decisions JSONB DEFAULT '[]',
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS tasks JSONB DEFAULT '[]',
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS directives JSONB DEFAULT '[]',
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS perspectives JSONB DEFAULT '[]',
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS questions_open JSONB DEFAULT '[]',
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS entities JSONB DEFAULT '[]',
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS topics JSONB DEFAULT '[]',
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS raw_ref TEXT,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS confidentiality TEXT DEFAULT 'open',
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS embedding vector(1536),
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS invalidated_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS supersedes UUID;
```

`kind IN ('note', 'memory', 'fact')`. Existing rows are `note`; distilled records are `memory`.

Alternative considered: separate `memories` table. Rejected because it duplicates infrastructure (search vectors, activity log, tags) and adds refactoring work in `routes/search.js`.

## Indexes

Beyond what's already there:
- B-tree on `(source, source_id)` for dedup on update
- B-tree on `occurred_at DESC` (chronological retrieval)
- GIN on `participants`, `entities`, `topics` (cross-source recall by person/project)
- GIN on `decisions`, `tasks`, `questions_open` JSONB
- IVFFlat on `embedding` (Pass B)

## Update semantics — invalidate, don't overwrite

For evolving threads (later message changes the decision; user revises a perspective), the wrong move is `UPDATE`. The right move:

1. New record with same `source_id`, link via `supersedes`
2. Old record gets `invalidated_at = NOW()`
3. Search filters out invalidated rows by default; opt-in with `?show_history=1`

This protects against silent preference drift, gives a decision-history log for free, and is the foundation for the *evolving perspectives + decisions* flavor of inference.

This is the Zep pattern, scaled down to Postgres. Pass C work.

## Cross-references

- The **insight engine** (Layer 3) reads memory records to find connections.
- The **user_model** (Layer 2) is built by aggregating perspectives and patterns across memory records over time.
- The **intents** table gets populated when the engine finds something needing action — referencing memory records via `related_memory_ids`.
- The **MCP server** exposes memory records to Claude Code via `search_memories`, `get_memory`, dimension-specific accessors like `get_open_loops`, `get_pending_commitments`.

title: AB Brain v2 — Understanding: user_model
content:
# Understanding Layer: user_model

## Why this layer exists

Most personal-AI products skip directly from memory (Layer 1) to inference (Layer 3). The result is generic productivity advice — "you have 4 meetings today" — because the system doesn't actually know *you*.

Layer 2 builds a continuous, evidence-backed model of the user. Without it:
- The coach (Layer 4) degrades into Apple-Wellness-style platitudes.
- The insight engine (Layer 3) surfaces "connections" obvious to anyone but uninteresting to you.
- Predictive features don't work — they need to know your patterns.

The user model is **load-bearing for everything above it.**

## Schema

```sql
CREATE TABLE user_model (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  category TEXT NOT NULL,              -- preference | pattern | working_style | identity | divergence
  topic TEXT,                          -- e.g. "meeting_style", "sleep", "decision_velocity"
  attribute TEXT NOT NULL,             -- the actual statement
  value JSONB,                         -- structured value if applicable
  confidence NUMERIC(3,2),             -- 0.0–1.0, calibrated by evidence count
  source TEXT NOT NULL,                -- stated | inferred | confirmed
  evidence_refs UUID[],                -- memory record ids supporting this
  first_observed TIMESTAMPTZ,
  last_observed TIMESTAMPTZ,
  last_updated TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
  invalidated_at TIMESTAMPTZ,          -- supersession when user changes
  supersedes UUID REFERENCES user_model(id)
);

CREATE INDEX idx_user_model_category ON user_model(category);
CREATE INDEX idx_user_model_topic ON user_model(topic);
CREATE INDEX idx_user_model_active ON user_model(invalidated_at) WHERE invalidated_at IS NULL;
```

## Categories

- **preference** — explicit tastes/wants. "Morning meetings preferred." "Async over realtime." "Decides slowly with multiple inputs." Source mostly `stated` or `confirmed`.
- **pattern** — observed behaviour. "Low task completion the day after a 4-hour-meeting block." "Energy dips on Thursdays." Source mostly `inferred`.
- **working_style** — how the user operates. "Reads pre-reads if delivered 48h ahead, skims if <24h." "Needs explicit closing-out — open loops eat attention."
- **identity** — facts. "ADHD." "Two kids." "Spouse name X." "Primary projects: A B C." Source `stated` + `confirmed`.
- **divergence** — stated-vs-revealed gaps. "Says wants async, joins live meetings." "Stated priority: deep work; revealed: 18 hrs/wk in meetings." This is the gold for the coach.

## How it's built

Continuous LLM pass, runs daily or per-N-new-memories:

1. Pull recent memory records (last 24h or 7 days).
2. Pull current user_model entries for relevant categories.
3. LLM prompt: "Here are recent memories and current model. What new attributes are evidenced? What existing attributes need confidence updates? What divergences exist?"
4. For each proposed change, write a new user_model row (with evidence_refs).
5. If superseding an existing entry, set `supersedes` and mark old row `invalidated_at`.

Confidence is calibrated:
- New attribute, single piece of evidence: 0.4–0.6 (tentative)
- Reinforced across N memories: rises toward 0.9
- Contradicted by newer evidence: drops, eventually invalidated
- Confirmed by user (explicit review UI): jumps to 1.0

## Surface for review

`/api/user-model` endpoint + a tab in the PWA: list active attributes, show evidence (clickable to memory records), let the user confirm / edit / remove. Implicit feedback also feeds back: every dismissal of an insight referencing a model attribute lowers that attribute's confidence.

## Privacy

Some attributes are sensitive (medical, relational, financial). Tag rows with `confidentiality` (same enum as memories: `open | confidential | restricted`). The coach reads `restricted` attributes for inference but never surfaces them in briefings or exposes via MCP without auth elevation.

## Where it sits in the build order

Pass C, alongside confidentiality tagging and invalidate-not-overwrite work. The coach (Layer 4) literally cannot be interesting until this exists, so the briefs that ship before this should be billed as "preview" — descriptive, not coaching.

title: AB Brain v2 — Inference: Insight Engine
content:
# Inference Layer: Insight Engine

## What it does

A periodic batch job that scans the brain (memories + user_model + metrics) looking for connections and patterns the user hasn't noticed and wouldn't think to ask about. Persists findings as `insights`. Surfaces them via daily/weekly briefs and (optionally) push notifications.

This is the layer that makes the system feel like a coach rather than a search engine.

## Six flavors of unseen connection

| Flavor | What it produces | Mechanism |
|---|---|---|
| **A. Same-topic recall** | "Related to what you're writing now: 3 prior items on Q2 you'd forgotten." | Embedding similarity (cosine on `embedding` column). Free once Pass B ships. |
| **B. Cross-entity synthesis** | "Alice, Bob, Charlie raised related concerns about Project X in separate threads. Worth a sync." | Entity-mention table + clustering. LLM synthesises top clusters. |
| **C. Behavioural / temporal patterns** | "Sleep dropped after the last 3 launch meetings. Next launch Monday." | Correlate metric series (sleep, meals, workouts, task completion) against event series (calendar, project events). |
| **D. Contradiction / gap detection** | "You told Alice budget is X, then Bob it's Y." / "Usually have a 1:1 with Bob every 2 weeks; haven't in 6." | Cross-memory consistency scan; calendar pattern gap detection. Requires invalidate-not-overwrite. |
| **E. Evolving perspectives + decisions** | "Your stance on X has shifted from Y to Z over the last 3 months." | Compare snapshots of `perspectives`/`decisions` over time on the same topic. |
| **F. Predictive** | "Board meeting in 3 days; you usually want pre-read by tomorrow EOD." | Reads user_model patterns + calendar + recurring events. Proposes intents. |

## Insights table

```sql
CREATE TABLE insights (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  kind TEXT NOT NULL CHECK (kind IN
    ('topic-link','entity-cluster','pattern','contradiction',
     'perspective-shift','prediction')),
  title TEXT NOT NULL,
  body TEXT NOT NULL,                  -- the LLM-generated explanation
  related_memory_ids UUID[],
  related_entities JSONB DEFAULT '[]',
  related_user_model_ids UUID[],       -- which model attributes informed this
  confidence NUMERIC(3,2),
  surfaced_at TIMESTAMPTZ,             -- when first shown to user
  user_feedback TEXT,                  -- 'useful' | 'noise' | 'wrong' | NULL
  acted_on BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
  superseded_by UUID,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_insights_unsurfaced ON insights(created_at) WHERE surfaced_at IS NULL;
CREATE INDEX idx_insights_kind ON insights(kind);
CREATE INDEX idx_insights_confidence ON insights(confidence DESC);
```

## Engine architecture

```
DAILY CRON (e.g. 6:30 AM)
    ↓
1. Pull candidate set: memories created/updated in last 24h
    ↓
2. For each candidate:
   a. Find related via embedding similarity (top-N from older memories)
   b. Find related via entity overlap
   c. Find related via temporal proximity
   → top-K cluster candidates
    ↓
3. For each high-scoring cluster:
   - Read user_model for relevant attributes
   - One LLM call: "here are N related items, the user model says Z,
     what's the non-obvious connection, why does it matter, what
     should the user do?"
   - LLM self-rates confidence
    ↓
4. Score, dedupe against existing unsurfaced insights
    ↓
5. Persist top-K to insights table
    ↓
6. Emit intents for high-confidence actionable insights
    ↓
7. Daily-brief skill (Layer 4) reads top insights, formats brief
```

Pattern detection (flavor C) and predictive (flavor F) run on different cadences — pattern detection weekly (needs more data per pass); predictive every few hours (calendar-driven).

## Conservative vs liberal surfacing — the unavoidable tension

Conservative = high confidence floor, fewer "wow" moments and fewer "that's silly" moments. Liberal = lower floor, more candidates, more hits and more noise.

ADHD + busy probably wants **liberal in collection, conservative in delivery**: engine generates many candidates (pennies of LLM cost), but the morning brief surfaces only the top 5–7 ranked. Push notifications reserved for time-sensitive only. Notification fatigue is the killer.

## Feedback loop

Every surfaced insight gets implicit signal (clicked? acted? dismissed?) and optional explicit signal (👍/👎 in the brief UI). Feed back into:
- Per-flavor confidence calibration (which flavors are most useful for this user)
- The user_model (consistent dismissals of an attribute lower its confidence)
- The prompt itself (LLM is shown its prior outputs + their feedback as few-shot examples)

## Cost discipline

- Only consider new items vs recent + user-model-relevant items
- Embedding-cluster first (free), LLM only on top candidates
- Cap LLM calls per run (e.g. 30/day across all flavors)
- Re-evaluate insights monthly: invalidated memories invalidate dependent insights

Realistic: ~30–60 LLM calls/day at gpt-4o-mini ≈ pennies. Larger models (gpt-4o or claude-sonnet) only for top 3 weekly insights ≈ cents/day.

## Surface for review

`/api/insights?since=YYYY-MM-DD` returns ranked unsurfaced insights. The daily-brief skill reads these. A separate PWA tab shows historical insights with feedback controls.

title: AB Brain v2 — Action: Intents + Claude Code Skills
content:
# Action Layer: Intents + Claude Code Skills

## The split

AB Brain emits **intents** — proposed actions with context, urgency, and a suggested skill. Claude Code (interactive sessions and a background-scheduled session) reads pending intents via MCP, runs the appropriate skill (with safety policy enforced), and writes outcomes back.

This means AB Brain doesn't need to build agent execution, draft-review UI, or undo logic. Claude Code already has tool use, permission prompts, undo-friendly defaults, and skills as the unit of capability. AB Brain's job in Layer 4 shrinks to: **detect conditions, emit intents, log outcomes.**

## The intents table

```sql
CREATE TABLE intents (
  id UUID PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
  kind TEXT NOT NULL,                  -- draft_email, calendar_block, follow_up, prep_digest, ...
  context TEXT NOT NULL,               -- human-readable summary for user/agent
  payload JSONB DEFAULT '{}',          -- structured args specific to the kind
  related_memory_ids UUID[],
  related_insight_id UUID,
  related_entities JSONB DEFAULT '[]',
  suggested_skill TEXT,
  tier TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'notify'
    CHECK (tier IN ('notify','suggest','draft','act_reversible','act_irreversible')),
  confidence NUMERIC(3,2),
  auto_act_allowed BOOLEAN DEFAULT false,
  urgency TEXT,                        -- immediate | today | this_week | someday
  expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  status TEXT DEFAULT 'pending'
    CHECK (status IN ('pending','acted','dismissed','expired','escalated')),
  acted_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  acted_outcome JSONB,
  acted_by TEXT,                       -- 'cron' | 'interactive'
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_intents_pending ON intents(urgency, created_at) WHERE status = 'pending';
CREATE INDEX idx_intents_by_skill ON intents(suggested_skill, status);
```

## Tier system

The single most important design choice in Layer 4. Each skill declares its tier; the policy enforcer gates auto-execution accordingly.

| Tier | Auto-act in cron? | Examples |
|---|---|---|
| **notify** | Yes | "Heads up: sleep dropped after launches." Just observation. |
| **suggest** | Yes (proposal) | "Want me to schedule recovery time Tuesday?" User clicks to convert. |
| **draft** | Yes (parked artifact) | Email drafts in Gmail Drafts. Calendar holds (not invites). |
| **act_reversible** | Yes (with rate limits) | Tag preferences, set reminders, log entries. Easily undone. |
| **act_irreversible** | **No — interactive only** | Send email, create calendar invite, delete. No exceptions. |

`auto_act_allowed` on the intent itself can also veto auto-execution even if tier permits.

## Per-skill safety constraints

Each skill declares:
- `max_actions_per_day` (e.g. `email-draft` ≤ 5/day)
- `confidence_floor` (e.g. skip if intent confidence < 0.7)
- `bail_out_behavior` (on unexpected state: queue for review and stop)

The policy enforcer is a small module between MCP and the skill runner. Reads `intents.tier`, `intents.confidence`, `intents.auto_act_allowed`, the skill's declared limits, and the user's session mode (cron vs interactive). Gates execution.

## MCP tool surface

A new MCP server (e.g. `mcp-abbrain`) exposes:

- `list_pending_intents(kind?, urgency?, max?)` → array
- `get_intent(id)` → full intent + related memories/insights
- `mark_intent_acted(id, outcome_json)`
- `dismiss_intent(id, reason)`
- `escalate_intent(id, reason)` — bumps to interactive review

Plus read-side tools so Claude Code can fetch context without going through intents:
- `search_memories(query, kind?, since?)`
- `get_memory(id)`
- `get_open_loops(scope?)`
- `get_user_model(category?, topic?)`
- `get_insights(since?, kind?)`

And write-side tools for skills that need to record findings back:
- `log_outcome(memory_id, outcome_json)`
- `update_user_model(attribute_id, change_json)`

## The six initial skills

| Skill | Tier | Trigger | Writes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `daily-brief` | notify | cron 7am | morning push notification + markdown digest |
| `weekly-brief` | notify | cron Sun evening | week-in-review + week-ahead structure |
| `meeting-prep-digest` | notify | 24h before each calendar event | markdown digest, optionally pushed |
| `open-loop-sweep` | notify | feeds daily-brief | triaged list of unresolved items |
| `email-draft` | draft | intents from memory `tasks` of kind "reply" | Gmail Drafts entry |
| `calendar-block` | suggest | pattern-derived (recovery/buffer) or explicit intent | calendar hold (not invite) |

Each is small (~100 LOC of skill manifest + prompt). Built one at a time when actually needed.

## Background-scheduled cron session

Runs at fixed times (e.g. 7am for daily, Sunday 6pm for weekly, hourly for meeting-prep checks). The session:

1. Calls `list_pending_intents()` filtered by allowed tiers + auto_act_allowed=true.
2. For each, runs the appropriate skill via the policy enforcer.
3. Logs outcomes via `mark_intent_acted` or `escalate_intent`.
4. Generates the brief output, pushes notification.
5. Exits.

Interactive sessions can run any tier (the user is in the loop).

## Mandatory daily review digest

Every morning brief includes a "what Claude Code did unattended in the last 24h" section:
- **Acted:** completed actions (with reversal links where possible)
- **Drafted-for-review:** artifacts parked for your decision
- **Queued / escalated:** intents that need interactive attention
- **Dismissed by policy:** intents the cron declined (with reason)

Without this, drift goes unchecked. With it, background-scheduled is safe to actually use.

## Where this lands

- New `routes/intents.js` for AB Brain side (CRUD + status updates).
- New repository `mcp-abbrain` (Node, uses MCP SDK) for the MCP server.
- New repo or `~/.claude/skills/` directory for the six custom skills.
- Cron / launchd / Railway scheduler runs the background Claude Code session.

title: AB Brain v2 — Coach: Posture and Prompt
content:
# Coach: Posture and Prompt Design

## What "act like a coach" means

A coach is not a search engine that responds; a coach has a point of view. The daily and weekly briefs become **evaluative**, not just descriptive:

- **Descriptive (default of most products):** "You have 4 meetings today, 3 overdue tasks, slept 5.5 hours."
- **Coaching:** "You said deep work was the priority this quarter. Calendar shows 18 hours of meetings this week and 2 hours of unbroken focus blocks. Same pattern as 6 weeks ago when Q1 milestone slipped. Want me to block tomorrow afternoon?"

The difference is reading the user's stated goals, comparing to revealed behaviour, and saying the uncomfortable thing.

## Decided: scope and tone

- **Scope: whole-life.** Work, health/wellness, relationships, personal events. Anything in the brain is fair game.
- **Tone: balanced.** Observes alignment between stated and revealed. Names misalignment without preaching. Suggests, doesn't lecture. Tracks its own prior observations and references them when patterns repeat.

Whole-life scope makes Pass C (privacy + confidentiality) genuinely load-bearing. The coach reads across work + health + relationships, so some inferences must never surface in a brief that someone else might glance at.

## Prompt template (starting point — will iterate)

```
You are <USER>'s coach. Today is <DATE>.

Read this user model:
<user_model entries, grouped by category>

Read these recent memories (last <N> days):
<memory records — focus on decisions, tasks, perspectives, questions>

Read these recent metrics:
<sleep, meals, workouts, task completion, calendar shape>

Read your prior observations to this user:
<last 7 daily-brief outputs, their feedback, whether acted on>

Compare stated goals/values to revealed behaviour.
- Where they align, acknowledge briefly (one sentence max).
- Where they diverge, name it directly but without judgement.
- Propose ONE concrete adjustment for today (or this week, for weekly).
- If you've flagged this same divergence before, say so and ask
  what's blocking action.

Constraints:
- Brief is readable in 60 seconds (≤ 250 words).
- No padding, no platitudes, no "great job!" If something is great,
  say what specifically and why it matters. Otherwise skip.
- Surface 5–7 items max, ranked by what matters today.
- Restricted-confidentiality content: use for context, never quote.

Output format: structured markdown with sections:
- TOP TODAY (1 sentence — the thing that matters most)
- AT YOUR CALENDAR (meetings + prep needs)
- OPEN LOOPS (commitments past due, unresolved questions)
- PATTERNS (alignment between stated and revealed; max 2)
- WHAT I DID UNATTENDED (acted, drafted, queued, dismissed)
```

## Why prompt design is the most important investment

Direct tone is easy to write ("just be pointed"). Gentle is easy ("just be supportive"). **Balanced is the hardest** — it has to read each situation. The first 4–6 weeks of running the coach will be tuning what it observes vs skips, when it pushes back vs lets something slide.

Iteration loop:
1. User reads brief, marks each item useful / noise / wrong.
2. Per-week tuning pass: feed back the marked output as few-shot examples in next week's prompt.
3. After ~6 weeks, prompt should be stable; further iteration only on systematic misses.

## Safety properties

- **Never quotes restricted content.** Reads it for context only. Output never includes verbatim text from `restricted` memories.
- **No medical advice.** Observes patterns, names them ("your sleep is correlating with stress about Project X"), but doesn't prescribe. Suggests "consider talking to your doctor" instead of "take more magnesium."
- **No relationship advice except observation.** "Haven't called your sister in 6 weeks" is observation. "You should call your sister and apologise" is over-reach.
- **Cites evidence.** Every observation backed by which memories / metrics support it. User can drill in.
- **Failure mode: silence over noise.** When the coach has nothing useful to say, it says less. A brief that's mostly "no notable patterns today" is a feature, not a failure.

## Read-write surface for the coach

The coach reads:
- `user_model` (active entries)
- `memories` (recent + entity-relevant + topic-relevant)
- `insights` (recent unsurfaced + pattern-related)
- `intents` (pending acted-on summary for the "what I did" section)
- Metric data (sleep, meals, workouts, task completion, calendar)

The coach writes:
- Brief output → push notification + persisted as a `coach_brief` memory record
- Optional `update_user_model` call when it discovers a confident new attribute
- Optional `dismiss_intent` for stale items

## Output channels

- **Push notification** (existing infra in `server.js:219–375`)
- **Email digest** (optional fallback)
- **PWA tab** for browsing brief history + giving feedback
- **MCP `get_latest_brief()`** so any Claude Code session can pull the morning brief

title: AB Brain v2 — Build Sequence + Open Questions
content:
# Build Sequence + Open Questions

## What's already shipped

**Track 1 (April 2026)** — security + integrity baseline. Purge endpoint hardened against SQL injection. Header-only auth (no `?api_key=` query string). Helmet CSP enabled. CORS allowlist via `CORS_ORIGINS`. Multi-table writes wrapped in transactions (`withTransaction` helper in `db.js`). All five fixes in PR #6, merged to master.

This is the trustworthy foundation everything below builds on.

## Pass A — Memory layer (months 1–2)

1. **Distillation primitive** (week 1). Manual paste-as-input. Iterate prompt on real samples.
2. **Gmail connector** (weeks 2–3). OAuth + nightly poll + dedup + push to distillation.
3. **Pre-filter + LLM scoring** (week 3). Tune with real Gmail data.
4. **Second connector — TBD** (weeks 4–6). M365 / Notion-read / Apple Notes / Apple Health.
5. **Other connectors** (months 2+). Same pattern, additive.

## Pass B — Inference foundation (months 2–3, overlaps with A)

1. **pgvector extension + embedding column** on memories.
2. **Backfill embeddings** for existing knowledge + transcripts via OpenAI text-embedding-3-small.
3. **Hybrid retrieval** in `routes/search.js` — blend tsvector_rank + cosine.
4. **Entity-mention table** — extract entities at distillation time, store with confidence.
5. **Insight engine cron** — six flavors implemented progressively (A and F first, others as data accrues).
6. **MCP server** stood up — read-side first (search_memories, get_open_loops), write-side second (log_outcome).

## Pass C — Understanding + privacy (months 3–4)

1. **`user_model` table** + continuous-update LLM job.
2. **Confidentiality tagging** on memories + user_model entries. `restricted` content bypasses OpenAI.
3. **Invalidate-don't-overwrite** semantics for memories and user_model. Foundation for evolving-perspectives flavor.
4. **PWA review surfaces** for user_model entries (confirm / edit / remove).

## Pass D — Action layer (month 3+, parallel with C)

1. **`intents` table** + policy enforcer module.
2. **MCP write-side tools** (mark_intent_acted, dismiss_intent).
3. **First skill: `daily-brief`** (notify tier — safest first ship).
4. **Second skill: `meeting-prep-digest`** (notify tier — high immediate value).
5. **Third + fourth skills** (`open-loop-sweep`, `email-draft`) once first two are stable.
6. **Background-scheduled cron session** wiring (launchd / cron / Railway scheduled job).
7. Coach posture rolls in once user_model has enough confidence to be useful.

## Open questions

| Question | When to decide | Default if undecided |
|---|---|---|
| Second Pass A connector | Before Pass A week 4 | Apple Notes via iOS Shortcuts |
| Cost ceiling for LLM calls | Before pre-filter tuning | $5/day soft cap; alert if exceeded |
| Backfill posture for historical content | After Pass A is stable | Last 90 days for Gmail; on-demand for older |
| Local-model lane for `restricted` content | During Pass C | Skip OpenAI for `restricted`; defer local-model implementation to Pass D+ |
| First Claude Code skill to ship | Pass D week 1 | `daily-brief` (lowest blast radius, highest daily value) |

## Decided architecture choices (recap)

- Interaction surfaces: Claude.ai chat, Claude Projects, Claude Code, AB Brain PWA. All read/write via MCP (PWA via direct REST).
- Trigger mode: **background-scheduled** by default with strict per-skill safety tiers
- Coach scope: **whole-life** (work + health + relationships)
- Coach tone: **balanced** (observes alignment, names misalignment, doesn't preach)
- Storage: extend existing `knowledge` table with `kind` discriminator + new columns (preferred over a separate `memories` table)
- Action layer: AB Brain emits intents; Claude Code + skills execute. Layer 4 is not built into AB Brain.

## What's NOT in v2 (intentionally deferred)

- Multi-user / sharing — single-user remains the assumption
- Offline-first / local-file storage — fights the server-of-truth model
- Folder hierarchies — categories + tags + entity links are enough
- Self-editing memory by autonomous agent — human-in-the-loop is the model
- Atomic-note discipline (Zettelkasten) — wrong friction profile for ADHD speed
- Native iOS app — PWA stays the mobile surface for now
- OpenClaw integration — explicitly excluded; no infrastructure or logic borrowed (see *What We Borrowed (and What We Didn't)*)

title: AB Brain v2 — What We Borrowed (and What We Didn't)
content:
# What We Borrowed (and What We Didn't)

This design pulls patterns from existing memory frameworks and second-brain tools. Patterns are **adapted to AB Brain's stack and goals, not copied wholesale.** This entry catalogs the borrowing so future contributors know what's adopted, what's deliberately rejected, and what's uniquely AB Brain v2.

## Borrowed (with adaptation)

### From mem0
- **Hybrid retrieval (vector + keyword fused).** mem0 fuses vector similarity, BM25, and entity matching. AB Brain v2 will blend tsvector full-text rank with cosine on a pgvector embedding column. *Lands in `routes/search.js` as Pass B.*
- **Atomic facts as the unit of memory.** Each distilled item produces structured fields, not free-text blobs. AB Brain extends this with richer dimensions (decisions, tasks, directives, perspectives, questions).

### From Letta (formerly MemGPT)
- **Memory taxonomy via metadata, not separate tables.** Letta uses memory blocks. AB Brain uses a `kind` discriminator + `confidence` field on the existing `knowledge` table — same logical effect without a schema rewrite.
- **Confidence tagging.** `unverified | tentative | confirmed` on every memory and user_model entry. Distilled facts default to tentative; user confirmation jumps to confirmed.

### From Zep
- **Soft-invalidate-don't-overwrite for evolving content.** When a later message changes a decision, the old record gets `invalidated_at` and the new record links via `supersedes`. Search filters out invalidated rows by default. Foundation for the evolving-perspectives flavor of inference.
- **Temporal validity (`valid_until`).** Some facts age out ("Bob is on the team" stops being true when he leaves). Optional column, useful especially for medical and relational data.
- **Lineage from raw to derived.** Every distilled memory keeps `raw_ref` pointing back to the source.

### From Logseq / Obsidian / Roam
- **Daily-notes-as-universal-capture.** Roam's insight: time is a more reliable organizing dimension than folders. AB Brain's `daily-brief` skill + iOS Shortcuts capture surface adopt this — the daily brief is a temporal index that surfaces relevant items by date and context.
- **Friction-tiered capture.** Hotkey + voice + share-sheet from Obsidian / Mem.ai. Lands as iOS Shortcuts → `/api/intake`.

### From PARA / Building a Second Brain (Tiago Forte)
- **CODE lifecycle (Capture-Organize-Distill-Express).** Maps directly to AB Brain's flow: intake → distillation pipeline → memory store → daily/weekly briefs. We don't use PARA's Project/Area/Resource/Archive folders — categories + tags + entity links are enough.

### From Khoj
- **PostgreSQL + pgvector as the core.** Validates the choice to stay on Postgres rather than adopting a separate vector DB.

## NOT borrowed (explicitly rejected)

### From OpenClaw
- **Nothing.** Earlier research on OpenClaw didn't survive a sanity check (the agent's findings on stars / repo details / branding didn't match anything verifiable). User explicitly excluded OpenClaw from the design. Zero infrastructure or logic borrowed.

### From mem0
- **Multi-backend vector/graph factory.** ElastiCache, Pinecone, Weaviate, Neo4j as pluggable backends. Architecturally elegant but operationally heavyweight. AB Brain is single-user; pgvector is enough.

### From Letta
- **Self-editing memory via agent tool calls.** Letta agents rewrite their own memory blocks. AB Brain wants human-in-the-loop — the user confirms, the agent doesn't autonomously update its model of the user.

### From Zep
- **Community subgraph clustering.** Useful at thousands of entities and dozens of multi-hop questions/day. AB Brain's scale doesn't justify the algorithmic overhead. Defer.

### From Logseq / Obsidian / Reor
- **Local-first file storage.** Beautiful for offline, fights everything else AB Brain has built. The stack is server-of-truth + PWA cache; CRDTs for concurrent edits would balloon the codebase.

### From Zettelkasten
- **Atomic-note discipline + manual link density.** Wrong friction profile for ADHD + speed. AB Brain's distilled records are larger units; entity links are inferred, not hand-typed.

### From traditional Obsidian
- **Folder hierarchies.** Categories + tags + entity links are enough. Mem.ai validates the no-folders posture.

## Uniquely AB Brain v2 (no clear precedent in researched systems)

### The four-layer split with Claude Code as Layer 4
Most personal-AI products try to build their own action engine. AB Brain delegates the action layer entirely to Claude Code, communicating via an `intents` table contract. This is a major scope reduction and doesn't appear in any system we studied.

### The intents table as the AB-Brain ↔ Claude-Code contract
A single table that's both the policy boundary (tier-gated) and the integration surface (MCP-exposed). No researched system has this exact pattern.

### Evolving perspectives + decisions as a first-class flavor of inference
The insight engine includes flavor E: "how the user's stated views and decisions on a topic shift over time." None of mem0, Letta, or Zep tracks the user's own evolution as a primary concern. They track facts about the world; AB Brain tracks how the user changes.

### Whole-life balanced coach backed by user_model
Most "AI coach" products are work-only or wellness-only with gentle / cheerleader tone. AB Brain v2's coach reads across work + health + relationships and uses a balanced tone — observation + accountability + suggested action — backed by an explicit user_model. The combination is rare.

### The signal/noise distillation pipeline as a first-class architectural layer
Most second-brain tools punt on ingestion ("the user manually files everything") or do naive ingestion (everything is stored, search figures it out later). AB Brain v2 treats the noise → signal → condensed-with-context pipeline as load-bearing because the user's real bottleneck is volume, not retrieval.

## Why this matters

Knowing what's borrowed and what's adapted protects against two failure modes:

1. **Cargo-culting** — implementing a pattern because someone famous did it, without checking it fits AB Brain's stack and goals.
2. **Reinventing the wheel** — solving problems other systems already solved well (hybrid retrieval, soft-invalidation, friction-tiered capture).

Future audits should compare AB Brain v2 against this catalog. If a pattern from another system would be useful and isn't in the rejected list, consider adopting it. If a pattern in AB Brain v2 doesn't trace back to either an entry above OR the "uniquely AB Brain" list, it may be unjustified.
